name: White Turnip Soup (Potage Aux Navets Blancs)
recipeIngredient: 4 slice To 5 slices of good-quality white bread, i.e., French, peasant
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt butter in a medium saucepan.
2. Add the coarsely chopped turnips and onions to the melted butter.
3. Saute over medium heat for about 5 minutes, stirring frequently, until the onions are translucent.
4. Bring 6 to 8 cups of water (depending on desired soup quantity and strength) to a boil.
5. Pour the boiling water into the saucepan with turnips and onions.
6. Add the bread slices (broken) to the saucepan.
7. Heat the mixture until it reaches a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for about 15 minutes or until the turnips are tender when pierced with a fork.
8. Remove from heat and let cool.
9. Season with salt and pepper if desired.
10. Transfer the cooled soup into a food processor or blender to blend, or press through a sieve using a wooden spoon.
11. Refrigerate for up to two days or freeze for several weeks before proceeding.
12. Before serving, reheat the soup gently without boiling and stir in the beaten egg yolks mixed with cream until blended and creamy.
13. Taste and adjust seasoning if necessary.
14. Serve hot in individual bowls garnished with chopped parsley or chives.
